description: Complete upper taxonomy for all military aircraft categories. Classifies aircraft by mission, role, platform type, flight principle, propulsion, autonomy, generation, operational environment, and every engineering-relevant axis.

MilitaryAircraft.label: Military Aircraft
MilitaryAircraft.description: Any aircraft designed, procured, or operated for military purposes including combat, support, training, logistics, surveillance, and special missions.

FixedWingAircraft.label: Fixed Wing Aircraft
FixedWingAircraft.description: Aircraft that generate lift by means of fixed aerofoil surfaces (wings). Aerodynamic lift is produced by forward motion relative to the air.

Fighter.label: Fighter Aircraft
Fighter.description: Aircraft optimized for air-to-air combat. Primary mission is gaining and maintaining air superiority by destroying enemy aircraft.

AirSuperiorityFighter.label: Air Superiority Fighter
AirSuperiorityFighter.description: Dedicated to achieving control of airspace by defeating all enemy air threats. Optimized for BVR and WVR engagements.

MultiroleFighter.label: Multirole Fighter
MultiroleFighter.description: Capable of performing both air-to-air and air-to-ground missions in a single sortie. Balances agility with strike payload.

Interceptor.label: Interceptor
Interceptor.description: Optimized for rapid climb and high-speed intercept of enemy aircraft or missiles. Often sacrifices maneuverability for speed and altitude.

StrikeFighter.label: Strike Fighter
StrikeFighter.description: Aircraft optimized for ground attack with retained air-to-air self-defense capability. Ground attack is primary mission.

NavalFighter.label: Naval Fighter
NavalFighter.description: Fighter designed for carrier operations. Includes structural reinforcement for catapult launch and arrested landing.

CATOBARFighter.label: CATOBAR Naval Fighter
CATOBARFighter.description: Carrier aircraft launched by steam or EMALS catapult, recovered via arresting wires.

STOBARFighter.label: STOBAR Naval Fighter
STOBARFighter.description: Short Take-Off But Arrested Recovery. Uses ski-jump ramp for launch, arrested landing for recovery.

STOVLFighter.label: STOVL Naval Fighter
STOVLFighter.description: Short Take-Off Vertical Landing. Can operate from short-deck carriers and amphibious assault ships without catapults or wires.

FifthGenerationFighter.label: Fifth Generation Fighter
FifthGenerationFighter.description: Aircraft incorporating stealth, AESA radar, sensor fusion, supercruise capability, and internal weapon carriage as design requirements.

SixthGenerationFighter.label: Sixth Generation Fighter
SixthGenerationFighter.description: Next-generation air dominance aircraft incorporating AI-enabled mission systems, directed energy weapons, optionally manned configurations, loyal wingman control, advanced stealth, and hypersonic missile integration.

Bomber.label: Bomber
Bomber.description: Aircraft designed to attack surface targets with large payloads of bombs, missiles, or other ordnance. Emphasizes payload, range, and penetration capability.

StrategicBomber.label: Strategic Bomber
StrategicBomber.description: Long-range bomber capable of striking targets deep in enemy territory, including nuclear delivery. Intercontinental range.

TacticalBomber.label: Tactical Bomber
TacticalBomber.description: Medium-range bomber for operational and tactical depth strikes. Less range than strategic bomber but higher precision.

StealthBomber.label: Stealth Bomber
StealthBomber.description: Bomber incorporating very low observable design to penetrate integrated air defense systems.

DiveBomber.label: Dive Bomber
DiveBomber.description: Historical aircraft designed to deliver bombs in a steep dive for accuracy. Mostly WWII era.

TorpedoBomber.label: Torpedo Bomber
TorpedoBomber.description: Aircraft designed to deliver aerial torpedoes against naval vessels. Historical role.

AttackAircraft.label: Attack Aircraft
AttackAircraft.description: Aircraft optimized for close air support and direct attack of ground targets in close proximity to friendly forces.

CloseAirSupport.label: Close Air Support Aircraft
CloseAirSupport.description: Designed for low-altitude, highly precise fire support to ground troops in direct contact with enemy. Requires loiter capability and robust communications.

Gunship.label: Gunship
Gunship.description: Fixed-wing aircraft converted or designed to carry heavy lateral-firing cannon and sensor systems for persistent armed overwatch.

LightAttack.label: Light Attack Aircraft
LightAttack.description: Low-cost, propeller or turboprop aircraft for COIN operations, armed overwatch, and light strike.

SEAD.label: SEAD Aircraft
SEAD.description: Suppression of Enemy Air Defense. Specialized aircraft or configured variant to detect and destroy radar, SAM, and air defense systems.

DEAD.label: DEAD Aircraft
DEAD.description: Destruction of Enemy Air Defense. Kinetic destruction of integrated air defense network nodes.

Reconnaissance.label: Reconnaissance Aircraft
Reconnaissance.description: Aircraft whose primary mission is collection of intelligence through photographic, electronic, or other sensor means.

StrategicReconnaissance.label: Strategic Reconnaissance
StrategicReconnaissance.description: High-altitude, long-range aircraft for national-level intelligence collection.

TacticalReconnaissance.label: Tactical Reconnaissance
TacticalReconnaissance.description: Shorter-range aircraft providing battlefield and operational intelligence.

ISR.label: Intelligence Surveillance Reconnaissance
ISR.description: Multi-sensor ISR platforms combining imaging, SIGINT, ELINT, and MASINT collection.

SIGINT.label: SIGINT Platform
SIGINT.description: Signals intelligence collection. Intercepts communications and electronic emissions.

ELINT.label: ELINT Platform
ELINT.description: Electronic intelligence. Catalogues radar emissions, frequencies, pulse patterns.

IMINT.label: IMINT Platform
IMINT.description: Imagery intelligence. EO, IR, and SAR imaging for ground target analysis.

MASINT.label: MASINT Platform
MASINT.description: Measurement and signature intelligence. Collects signatures of weapons and systems.

HUMINT_Support.label: HUMINT Support Platform
HUMINT_Support.description: Supports human intelligence operations through covert insertion, extraction, or communications relay.

MaritimePatrol.label: Maritime Patrol Aircraft
MaritimePatrol.description: Long-endurance aircraft for maritime domain awareness, ASW, and surface search.

ASW.label: Anti-Submarine Warfare Aircraft
ASW.description: Specialized in locating, tracking, and destroying enemy submarines using sonobuoys, MAD, and torpedoes.

SurfaceSearch.label: Surface Search Aircraft
SurfaceSearch.description: Detection and classification of surface vessels.

AEW.label: Airborne Early Warning
AEW.description: Aircraft carrying airborne radar to provide early warning of incoming aircraft, missiles, and surface threats beyond ground radar horizon.

AEWC.label: AEW&C Aircraft
AEWC.description: Airborne Early Warning and Control. Combines early warning with battlespace management and datalink distribution.

AEW.label: AEW Only
AEW.description: Early warning only. No command and control role.

ElectronicWarfareAircraft.label: Electronic Warfare Aircraft
ElectronicWarfareAircraft.description: Aircraft whose primary payload is electronic warfare systems: jamming, SIGINT, ELINT, electronic attack, and electronic protection.

StandoffJammer.label: Standoff Jammer
StandoffJammer.description: Jamming aircraft operating outside threat rings to suppress enemy radar and communications.

EscortJammer.label: Escort Jammer
EscortJammer.description: EW aircraft that accompany strike packages to suppress defenses along the route and in target area.

SignalsJammer.label: Communications Jammer
SignalsJammer.description: Specialized in jamming enemy tactical and strategic communications.

Tanker.label: Tanker Aircraft
Tanker.description: Aircraft that provide aerial refueling to extend the range and endurance of receiver aircraft.

StrategicTanker.label: Strategic Tanker
StrategicTanker.description: Large capacity tanker for strategic force projection.

TacticalTanker.label: Tactical Tanker
TacticalTanker.description: Smaller tankers for shorter-range tactical refueling.

BuddyRefueling.label: Buddy Refueling Aircraft
BuddyRefueling.description: Tactical aircraft fitted with centerline refueling pods to serve as impromptu tankers.

Transport.label: Transport Aircraft
Transport.description: Aircraft for moving personnel, equipment, and supplies.

StrategicAirlifter.label: Strategic Airlifter
StrategicAirlifter.description: Very large, intercontinental range cargo aircraft. Moves heavy outsized cargo across strategic distances.

TacticalAirlifter.label: Tactical Airlifter
TacticalAirlifter.description: Medium aircraft for intratheater airlift, capable of operating from short unprepared strips.

LightTransport.label: Light Transport
LightTransport.description: Small transport for liaison, medevac, and utility roles.

VIPTransport.label: VIP / Executive Transport
VIPTransport.description: Configured for senior leadership transport with communications, security, and comfort.

MedicalEvacuation.label: Medical Evacuation Transport
MedicalEvacuation.description: Aircraft configured for patient transport and in-flight medical treatment.

AirdropAircraft.label: Airdrop Aircraft
AirdropAircraft.description: Configured for parachute delivery of personnel and equipment.

Trainer.label: Trainer Aircraft
Trainer.description: Aircraft used for initial pilot training, advanced combat training, and weapons instruction.

PrimaryTrainer.label: Primary Trainer
PrimaryTrainer.description: Initial flight training. Basic aircraft handling and airmanship.

JetTrainer.label: Jet Trainer
JetTrainer.description: Advanced jet familiarization and combat lead-in training.

CombatTrainer.label: Lead-In Fighter Trainer
CombatTrainer.description: Light combat aircraft also used as lead-in trainer to advanced fighters.

SpecialMissionAircraft.label: Special Mission Aircraft
SpecialMissionAircraft.description: Aircraft configured for specific specialized roles beyond standard combat categories.

CommandPost.label: Airborne Command Post
CommandPost.description: Airborne command and control centers for national leadership and battlefield command.

BattlefieldCoordinator.label: Battlefield Coordinator
BattlefieldCoordinator.description: Airborne battle management and control for ground and air forces.

SearchAndRescue.label: Combat Search and Rescue
SearchAndRescue.description: Recovery of downed aircrew behind enemy lines.

PsychologicalOperations.label: Psychological Operations Aircraft
PsychologicalOperations.description: Broadcasts propaganda, leaflets, and communications disruption.

SpecialOperations.label: Special Operations Aircraft
SpecialOperations.description: Insertion, extraction, and support of special operations forces.

CalibrationAircraft.label: Calibration Aircraft
CalibrationAircraft.description: Checks and calibrates ground-based navigation and radar systems.

ExperimentalAircraft.label: Experimental Aircraft
ExperimentalAircraft.description: Technology demonstrators and experimental platforms not intended for operational service in their tested configuration.

HypersonicTestVehicle.label: Hypersonic Test Vehicle
HypersonicTestVehicle.description: Validates hypersonic flight regimes, materials, and propulsion.

Spaceplane.label: Spaceplane
Spaceplane.description: Aircraft capable of reaching orbital or suborbital altitudes.

RotaryWingAircraft.label: Rotary Wing Aircraft
RotaryWingAircraft.description: Aircraft that generate lift through rotating blades (rotors). Capable of vertical takeoff and landing, hover, and low-speed flight.

AttackHelicopter.label: Attack Helicopter
AttackHelicopter.description: Dedicated armed helicopter for anti-armor, close air support, and escort missions.

DedicatedAttack.label: Dedicated Attack Helicopter
DedicatedAttack.description: Purpose-built attack helicopter with tandem crew, FLIR, fire control, and heavy armament.

ArmedUtilityHelicopter.label: Armed Utility Helicopter
ArmedUtilityHelicopter.description: Utility helicopter with weapon systems added.

UtilityHelicopter.label: Utility Helicopter
UtilityHelicopter.description: Multi-purpose helicopter for troop transport, sling load, medevac, and general support.

HeavyLiftHelicopter.label: Heavy Lift Helicopter
HeavyLiftHelicopter.description: Very large helicopters for outsized cargo and heavy external load operations.

NavalHelicopter.label: Naval Helicopter
NavalHelicopter.description: Helicopter designed for shipborne operations: ASW, surface search, torpedo delivery.

ASWHelicopter.label: ASW Helicopter
ASWHelicopter.description: Anti-submarine warfare helicopter with sonobuoys, dipping sonar, and torpedoes.

UtilityNavalHelicopter.label: Naval Utility Helicopter
UtilityNavalHelicopter.description: General purpose naval helicopter for VERTREP, SAR, and personnel transport.

ReconHelicopter.label: Reconnaissance Helicopter
ReconHelicopter.description: Optimized for battlefield observation and target designation.

TrainingHelicopter.label: Training Helicopter
TrainingHelicopter.description: Helicopter for rotary wing qualification training.

SARHelicopter.label: SAR / CSAR Helicopter
SARHelicopter.description: Combat and non-combat search and rescue.

MedevacHelicopter.label: Medevac Helicopter
MedevacHelicopter.description: Medical evacuation helicopter with patient treatment capability.

NavalMineCountermeasures.label: Helicopter MCM
NavalMineCountermeasures.description: Airborne mine sweeping and mine neutralization.

TiltrotorAircraft.label: Tiltrotor Aircraft
TiltrotorAircraft.description: Aircraft that combine fixed-wing cruise efficiency with rotorcraft VTOL capability through tilting proprotor systems.

AttackTiltrotor.label: Attack Tiltrotor (Concept)
AttackTiltrotor.description: Proposed armed tiltrotor combining attack helicopter capability with tiltrotor speed.

UAV.label: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle
UAV.description: Aircraft without an onboard human crew. Controlled remotely, semi-autonomously, or fully autonomously.

NanoUAV.label: Nano UAV
NanoUAV.description: Smallest class. Weight under 250g. Hand-launched, short endurance.

MiniUAV.label: Mini / Small UAV
MiniUAV.description: 2kg to 25kg. Company and battalion level ISR.

TacticalUAV.label: Tactical UAV
TacticalUAV.description: Brigade to division level ISR. Runway or catapult launched.

MALE.label: Medium Altitude Long Endurance UAV
MALE.description: Operates at 10,000-50,000ft for 24+ hours. Theater-level ISR and strike.

HALE.label: High Altitude Long Endurance UAV
HALE.description: Operates above 60,000ft for 36+ hours. Strategic ISR.

UCAV.label: Unmanned Combat Air Vehicle
UCAV.description: Autonomous or semi-autonomous combat aircraft without crew.

StealthUCAV.label: Stealth UCAV
StealthUCAV.description: Low-observable UCAV for penetrating defended airspace.

PropellerUCAV.label: Propeller UCAV
PropellerUCAV.description: Turboprop UCAV for persistent armed overwatch.

LoyalWingman.label: Loyal Wingman / Collaborative Combat Aircraft
LoyalWingman.description: Unmanned aircraft designed to fly as force multiplier alongside manned fighter.

LoiteringMunition.label: Loitering Munition / Kamikaze UAV
LoiteringMunition.description: Expendable UAV that loiters and dives on target. Single-use weapon.

SwarmUAV.label: Swarm UAV
SwarmUAV.description: Large numbers of small coordinated UAVs operating under collective intelligence.

RelayUAV.label: Communications Relay UAV
RelayUAV.description: Acts as communications node for extending BLOS communications.

TargetDrone.label: Target Drone
TargetDrone.description: Simulates enemy aircraft and missiles for weapon testing and aircrew training.

DecoyUAV.label: Decoy UAV
DecoyUAV.description: Mimics radar signature of aircraft to saturate and deceive enemy defenses.

HypersonicAircraft.label: Hypersonic Aircraft
HypersonicAircraft.description: Aircraft capable of sustained flight above Mach 5.

classification_by_generation.label: Aircraft Generation Classification
classification_by_generation.description: Fighter aircraft generations by capabilities and technology.

STOL.label: Short Takeoff and Landing
STOL.description: Operations from very short runways or unprepared strips.

VTOL.label: Vertical Takeoff and Landing
VTOL.description: Aircraft that can take off and land vertically.

STOVL.label: Short Takeoff Vertical Landing
STOVL.description: Can take off from a short run but land vertically.

CATOBAR.label: Catapult Assisted Takeoff Barrier Arrested Recovery
CATOBAR.description: Carrier operations using catapult for launch and arresting wire for recovery.

STOBAR.label: Short Takeoff But Arrested Recovery
STOBAR.description: Ski-jump launch, arrested wire recovery. No catapult.

EMALS.label: Electromagnetic Launch System
EMALS.description: Electric catapult replacing steam catapult on CVN-78 class.

RocketAssisted.label: JATO / RATO Assisted
RocketAssisted.description: Rocket motors assist takeoff from short strips.

LowObservable.label: Low Observable
LowObservable.description: Radar cross section reduced by shaping and materials but not optimized.

VeryLowObservable.label: Very Low Observable
VeryLowObservable.description: Full stealth design with internal weapons, RAM coatings, shaping optimized for all radar bands.

AdaptiveStealth.label: Adaptive Stealth
AdaptiveStealth.description: Active systems that modify radar return dynamically.

RemotelyPiloted.label: Remotely Piloted
RemotelyPiloted.description: Human in the loop via datalink.

SemiAutonomous.label: Semi-Autonomous
SemiAutonomous.description: AI handles portions of mission; human supervises.

FullyAutonomous.label: Fully Autonomous
FullyAutonomous.description: No human in the loop for mission execution.

SwarmIntelligence.label: Swarm / Collective Intelligence
SwarmIntelligence.description: Multiple autonomous agents operating under coordinated AI.
